data.title: North Korean Operatives Steal Over $500 Million from DeFi Platforms in Three Weeks
data.description: North Korean-linked operatives have reportedly stolen more than $500 million from decentralized finance (DeFi) platforms in less than three weeks, raising the regime's estimated total crypto haul to $6.75 billion. According to NS3.AI, LayerZero reported that the April 18 KelpDAO exploit resulted in losses of approximately $290 million, with preliminary forensic analysis suggesting involvement from TraderTraitor within the Lazarus Group. Additionally, Drift Protocol suffered an estimated $286 million loss on April 1, with Elliptic noting that the laundering patterns of the exploit matched established attack vectors associated with the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK). The Ketman Project has indicated that around 100 North Korean operatives are embedded within blockchain companies.

data.title: Bipartisan push forward the PACE bill, which aims to open the Federal Reserve's payment network to non-banks.
data.description: The PACE Act, introduced by bipartisan lawmakers in the United States, aims to allow compliant non-bank payment institutions direct access to the Federal Reserve's payment system, garnering support from the crypto industry. The bill will establish a federal framework overseen by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, providing a unified registration path for payment companies and requiring them to maintain 1:1 reserves and meet risk control and record-keeping compliance requirements. Eligible institutions will be able to access core payment networks such as Fedwire, FedNow, and FedACH. The legislative goal is to reduce payment costs, improve speed and reliability, and make the transfer experience as simple as sending a text message. Industry observers believe this move could break the traditional banks' monopoly on underlying payment infrastructure, opening a crucial channel for stablecoins and crypto payment companies, and further promoting openness and competition in the financial system.

data.title: The New York Attorney General has filed charges against Coinbase and Gemini, alleging that prediction markets constitute "illegal gambling."
data.description: Letitia James has filed a lawsuit against Coinbase and Gemini, accusing them of violating New York State law by offering "disguised gambling" services through their prediction market platforms. Regulators argue that such event-based transactions (like sports or elections) are essentially gambling, particularly questioning the allowance for users aged 18 to 21, while New York law stipulates a minimum age of 21 for sports betting. Prosecutors are seeking substantial fines and forfeiture of proceeds, including at least $2.2 billion from Coinbase and at least $1.2 billion from Gemini. Neither company has officially responded, but Coinbase stated that prediction markets are regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, implying that their legality falls under federal jurisdiction.

data.title: SoFi launched XRP deposits, but faced user criticism due to restrictions on withdrawals.
data.description: SoFi announced that its crypto service supports XRP deposits, but the lack of support for withdrawals to external wallets has sparked user dissatisfaction. Currently, users can only hold or trade XRP within the platform and cannot transfer it to on-chain wallets. This design has been criticized by some users as an "ETF-like model," arguing that it only provides price exposure without truly integrating into the crypto ecosystem. SoFi stated that it will open the withdrawal function in the future, but has not yet provided a specific timeline.

data.title: SpaceX's AI Data Center Plans Face Feasibility Challenges
data.description: SpaceX has revealed that its plans for an AI data center in orbit, as well as infrastructure on the Moon and Mars, involve unverified technologies that may not be commercially viable. According to Jin10, the company acknowledged the potential challenges in achieving these ambitious goals, citing the need for further validation of the technologies involved. The disclosure raises questions about the feasibility of SpaceX's extraterrestrial projects, which aim to expand human presence beyond Earth. The company continues to explore innovative solutions to overcome these hurdles and advance its vision for space exploration.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Unauthorized Access to Anthropic's Mythos Model Reported
data.description: According to Jin10, market sources have reported that Anthropic's Mythos model was accessed by unauthorized users. Anthropic has stated that there is currently no evidence indicating that this illegal access has impacted their systems.

data.title: Iran Maintains Maritime Blockade Amid Hostilities
data.description: Iran has not requested an extension of the ceasefire, according to BlockBeats, citing multiple sources from Iran's Tasnim News Agency on April 22. The continuation of the maritime blockade signifies ongoing hostilities, and as long as the blockade persists, Iran is unlikely to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. If necessary, Iran may resort to force to break the blockade.

data.title: South Korea's March PPI Rises by 1.6%
data.description: South Korea's Producer Price Index (PPI) for March increased by 1.6%, according to Jin10. The previous month's figure was revised to 0.6%.

data.title: Complex Yield Strategy Evolves into Interlinked Exposures
data.description:
A straightforward yield strategy has transformed into a complex web of interconnected exposures. According to Bloomberg, this development highlights the intricate nature of financial strategies that initially appear simple but can evolve into multifaceted systems. The shift from simplicity to complexity in yield strategies underscores the dynamic nature of financial markets, where initial plans can lead to unforeseen interdependencies and risks.

The evolution of these strategies reflects the broader trend in financial markets, where investors seek higher returns through increasingly sophisticated methods. As these strategies grow in complexity, they often involve multiple layers of exposure, creating a network of financial instruments that are interlinked. This interconnectedness can amplify risks, as changes in one part of the system may have cascading effects throughout the entire structure. The transformation of yield strategies into complex systems is a testament to the innovative approaches employed by investors in their quest for profitability.

The implications of this shift are significant, as it requires investors to have a deeper understanding of the potential risks and rewards associated with their strategies. The interconnected nature of these exposures means that investors must be vigilant in monitoring the various components of their portfolios to mitigate potential risks. As financial markets continue to evolve, the ability to navigate these complex systems will be crucial for investors aiming to achieve their financial goals while managing risk effectively.

data.title: Bond Market Volatility Expected to Decline Amid US-Iran Tensions
data.description: Traders are increasingly betting on a decrease in bond market volatility, despite ongoing tensions between the United States and Iran. Bloomberg posted on X that the anticipation of reduced fluctuations comes as the prospect of a peace agreement between the two nations remains uncertain. Market participants are closely monitoring geopolitical developments, which continue to influence investor sentiment and trading strategies. The bond market has experienced significant volatility in recent months, driven by various global events and economic indicators. However, traders are now positioning themselves for a potential stabilization, reflecting a shift in market dynamics. The situation between the US and Iran remains a critical factor in shaping future market movements, with any developments likely to impact volatility levels.

data.title: Analyst ColdBloodedShiller: Bitcoin Near $75,400 Signals Short Setup
data.description: Bitcoin is currently positioned near $75,400, which analyst ColdBloodedShiller identifies as a potential short setup. According to NS3.AI, this follows a rejection at the $78,000 level earlier in the week. The analyst suggests that a decline below $74,500 would indicate a shift in short-term momentum.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 630 USDT with a Narrowed 0.13% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 21, 2026, 20:55 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 630 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 630 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.13% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: U.S. API Reports Significant Drop in Refined Oil Stocks
data.description: The American Petroleum Institute (API) reported a substantial decrease in U.S. refined oil inventories for the week ending April 17. According to Jin10, the stocks fell by 4.59 million barrels, surpassing the expected decline of 2.5 million barrels. The previous week's data showed a reduction of 3.363 million barrels. This larger-than-anticipated drop in inventories may influence market dynamics and oil prices.

data.title: U.S. Extends Ceasefire with Iran Amid Proposal Awaiting
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the United States will extend its ceasefire with Iran as it awaits a unified proposal from Iranian leaders. According to NS3.AI, Trump emphasized that the U.S. military will maintain the blockade and remain fully prepared during this pause in hostilities.

data.title: Meta Platforms Launches $1 Billion Data Center Project in Tulsa
data.description: Meta Platforms has initiated a $1 billion data center construction project in Tulsa, Oklahoma. According to Jin10, the Tulsa data center project includes over $25 million in local infrastructure improvements. It is expected to support more than 1,000 construction jobs at its peak and create approximately 100 operational positions upon completion.

data.title: Trump Endorses Businessman for New York House Seat
data.description: A businessman who gained attention last year with a notable “Vote for Trump” sign has received an endorsement from U.S. President Donald Trump. Bloomberg posted on X that the endorsement is for the race to fill Elise Stefanik’s House seat in upstate New York.

data.title: Ripple Plans Quantum-Ready Transition for XRP Ledger by 2028
data.description: Ripple has outlined a roadmap to prepare the XRP Ledger for quantum computing challenges by 2028. According to NS3.AI, the company has already initiated testing to ensure quantum resistance. The plan involves integrating quantum-resistant methods into the test network alongside existing security measures by late 2026, with a complete transition expected by 2028. Ripple has also announced an emergency fallback mechanism to disable traditional signatures should quantum risks emerge earlier than anticipated.

data.title: AI-Driven Scams Surge 500%, Experts Urge Countermeasures
data.description: AI-enabled scam activities have surged by 500% over the past year, according to witnesses at a recent congressional hearing. Experts highlighted that this increase is complicating the detection of crypto fraud. According to NS3.AI, authorities are being urged to leverage AI technology to combat these sophisticated attackers. One witness proposed that existing laws be reviewed to potentially apply terrorism designation powers to ransomware actors who target hospitals.

data.title: Polymarket Seeks Funding at $15 Billion Valuation Amid Competitive Landscape
data.description: Polymarket is reportedly in discussions to secure funding at a valuation of $15 billion. According to NS3.AI, this move comes as its competitor, Kalshi, was recently valued at $22 billion. Industry experts suggest that Polymarket's anticipated token launch and potential airdrop farming could complicate the interpretation of its trading volume as purely product demand.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 75,000 USDT with a 1.76% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 21, 2026, 19:43 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 75,000 USDT and is now trading at 74,910.6875 USDT, with a narrowed 1.76% decrease in 24 hours.
